description: Animate Muller paleogeographic plate polygons across 0–750 Ma on a deck.gl 3D globe with play/pause and timeline scrubber

userStories.title: Scaffold Vite + React + TypeScript project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working Vite + React + TypeScript project scaffold so that all subsequent stories have a compile-and-run baseline to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists with scripts: dev, build, preview, typecheck, lint
tsconfig.json exists with strict mode enabled and JSX set to react-jsx
vite.config.ts exists with @vitejs/plugin-react configured
index.html exists referencing src/main.tsx
src/main.tsx renders <App /> into #root
src/App.tsx exists and renders a placeholder <div>
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
`npx vite build` completes without errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Use `npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts` as starting point. Dependencies: react, react-dom, typescript, @vitejs/plugin-react, vite. Dev server entry: src/main.tsx. All files go in C:/Users/ove.sundal/WebstormProjects/PlateTectonicPlayer/.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: package.json, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.app.json, tsconfig.node.json, vite.config.ts, index.html, src/main.tsx, src/App.tsx, eslint.config.js.

userStories.title: Add deck.gl and render static GlobeView
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Globe component that renders an interactive 3D globe using deck.gl GlobeView so that subsequent stories can layer plate polygon data on top.
acceptanceCriteria: deck.gl and @deck.gl/react are listed as dependencies in package.json
@types/geojson is listed as a dev dependency
src/components/Globe.tsx exists and exports a Globe React component
Globe renders a DeckGL component with views={[new GlobeView()]} and a blue-tinted basemap (or solid color background)
Globe accepts optional props: layers (DeckGL Layer array), initialViewState
Globe fills its parent container (width: 100%, height: 100%)
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
Dev server shows a 3D globe with no console errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Install: `npm install deck.gl @deck.gl/react`. Use DeckGL from '@deck.gl/react', GlobeView from 'deck.gl'. GlobeView initialViewState: { longitude: 0, latitude: 20, zoom: 1 }. For basemap, use a SimpleMeshLayer with a sphere or a solid background color — avoid Mapbox to keep it dependency-free. File to create: src/components/Globe.tsx.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Note: GlobeView is exported as _GlobeView in deck.gl v9 (experimental). Import as `import { _GlobeView as GlobeView } from 'deck.gl'`.

userStories.title: Create preprocess_muller.py data pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Python script that converts raw Muller GPKG/Shapefile data into per-timestep GeoJSON snapshots so that the browser app can load static files without a backend.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/preprocess_muller.py exists
Script accepts --input (path to Muller GPKG or Shapefile) and --output (target directory) CLI arguments
Script iterates ages 0 to 750 in steps of 5 Ma and writes plates_{N}Ma.geojson for each age
Each output GeoJSON is a valid FeatureCollection with plate polygon features
Script prints progress (age being processed) and final file count
Script uses geopandas and fiona (listed in scripts/requirements.txt)
A README comment at the top of the script explains how to run it
Script does not crash on missing ages — logs a warning and skips
userStories.technicalNotes: Use geopandas to read the Muller GPKG. Filter by age column (e.g., 'age' or 'reconstruction_age' depending on schema). Export each age slice as GeoJSON with geopandas .to_file(path, driver='GeoJSON'). Output path: public/data/muller/plates_{N}Ma.geojson. Create scripts/requirements.txt with geopandas, fiona, click. Files to create: scripts/preprocess_muller.py, scripts/requirements.txt.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: scripts/preprocess_muller.py, scripts/requirements.txt.

userStories.title: Define TypeScript types for plate data
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared TypeScript types for plate GeoJSON data and animation state so that all hooks and components have a single source of truth for data shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types/plates.ts exists and exports PlateProperties, PlateFeature, PlateFeatureCollection, AnimationState
PlateFeature extends GeoJSON.Feature<MultiPolygon | Polygon, PlateProperties>
AnimationState has fields: isPlaying (boolean), currentAge (number, 0–750 Ma), speed (number, Ma per real second)
Constants exported: AGE_MIN=0, AGE_MAX=750, AGE_STEP=5
No imports from non-existent modules — only imports from 'geojson' (type-only)
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
userStories.technicalNotes: File to create: src/types/plates.ts. Import types from 'geojson' (already available via @types/geojson installed in US-002). Use `import type` for all GeoJSON imports to avoid runtime overhead.

userStories.title: Implement usePlateData hook (fetch + cache)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a usePlateData hook that fetches and caches GeoJSON plate data per timestep so that the animation loop can request data for any age without redundant network requests.
acceptanceCriteria: src/hooks/usePlateData.ts exists and exports usePlateData
Hook signature: usePlateData(currentAge: number): { data: PlateFeatureCollection | null; isLoading: boolean }
Hook fetches /data/muller/plates_{N}Ma.geojson where N is the nearest AGE_STEP multiple to currentAge
Fetched results are cached in a module-level Map<number, PlateFeatureCollection> — no re-fetches for cached ages
Hook prefetches the next 2 timesteps (currentAge + AGE_STEP, currentAge + 2*AGE_STEP) when data for currentAge loads
isLoading is true only while the fetch for currentAge is in flight
Fetch errors are caught — hook returns data: null and isLoading: false on error
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
userStories.technicalNotes: File to create: src/hooks/usePlateData.ts. Use useState + useEffect for fetch lifecycle. Cache as module-level const (outside component): const cache = new Map<number, PlateFeatureCollection>(). Prefetch by calling fetch() without awaiting inside useEffect (fire-and-forget into cache). URL pattern: `/data/muller/plates_${age}Ma.geojson`. Import PlateFeatureCollection, AGE_STEP, AGE_MAX from src/types/plates.ts.

userStories.title: Render GeoJsonLayer plate polygons on globe
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see plate polygon outlines on the 3D globe for a given age so that the data pipeline and rendering stack are end-to-end verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Globe.tsx accepts a data prop of type PlateFeatureCollection | null
When data is provided, a GeoJsonLayer renders plate polygon outlines on the globe
GeoJsonLayer uses stroked: true, filled: false (outline-only style) with a visible color
When data is null, no GeoJsonLayer is rendered (or an empty one — no crash)
App.tsx passes data from usePlateData(0) to Globe at age 0 Ma for initial render verification
Dev server shows plate polygon outlines on the globe at present-day (0 Ma)
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Modify src/components/Globe.tsx to accept data prop. Import GeoJsonLayer from 'deck.gl'. Layer config: new GeoJsonLayer({ id: 'plates', data, stroked: true, filled: false, getLineColor: [255, 200, 0, 200], lineWidthMinPixels: 1 }). Pass layers={data ? [plateLayer] : []} to DeckGL. Also update src/App.tsx to call usePlateData(0) and pass result to Globe. Files to modify: src/components/Globe.tsx, src/App.tsx.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Globe.tsx updated with data prop + GeoJsonLayer.

userStories.title: Implement useAnimation hook (rAF play/pause)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a useAnimation hook that drives the geological age forward over real time using requestAnimationFrame so that the animation is smooth and frame-rate-aware.
acceptanceCriteria: src/hooks/useAnimation.ts exists and exports useAnimation
Hook signature: useAnimation(speed?: number): { isPlaying: boolean; currentAge: number; play: () => void; pause: () => void; seek: (age: number) => void }
Default speed is 30 Ma per second of real time
When playing, currentAge advances using requestAnimationFrame (delta time between frames in ms)
When currentAge reaches AGE_MAX (750), animation stops and isPlaying becomes false
play() starts the rAF loop; pause() cancels it via cancelAnimationFrame
seek(age) sets currentAge to age (clamped to [AGE_MIN, AGE_MAX]) and does not affect isPlaying state
rAF handle is stored in useRef and cancelled on component unmount
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
userStories.technicalNotes: File to create: src/hooks/useAnimation.ts. Use useRef<number | null> for rAF handle and useRef<number> for lastTimestamp. State: useState for isPlaying and currentAge. rAF callback: compute delta = now - lastTimestamp, advance age by speed * delta / 1000, clamp to [AGE_MIN, AGE_MAX]. Import AGE_MIN, AGE_MAX, AnimationState from src/types/plates.ts.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. File: src/hooks/useAnimation.ts. Also exposes speed and setSpeed for US-010.

userStories.title: Create TimelineControls component
userStories.description: As a user, I want play/pause and timeline scrubber controls so that I can control the animation and jump to any geological age.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/TimelineControls.tsx exists and exports TimelineControls
Component renders a Play/Pause toggle button that switches label based on isPlaying prop
Component renders an HTML range input (scrubber) with min=0, max=750, step=5
Scrubber value reflects currentAge prop
Changing the scrubber calls onSeek(newAge) prop
Clicking Play/Pause calls onPlay() or onPause() based on current isPlaying state
Component also renders current age as text (e.g., '250 Ma')
Component is styled to overlay at the bottom of the screen (absolute or fixed positioning)
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
userStories.technicalNotes: File to create: src/components/TimelineControls.tsx. Props interface: { isPlaying: boolean; currentAge: number; onPlay: () => void; onPause: () => void; onSeek: (age: number) => void }. Use inline styles or a CSS module for bottom overlay positioning. The age label should be prominent — large font, light color on dark background.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. File: src/components/TimelineControls.tsx. Speed selector added ahead-of-schedule for US-010.

userStories.title: Wire App.tsx — connect animation, data, globe, controls
userStories.description: As a user, I want the full animation experience where clicking Play animates plate polygons across 0–750 Ma on the 3D globe with the timeline scrubber tracking position.
acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx uses useAnimation() and usePlateData(currentAge) hooks
Globe component receives plate data for the current animated age
TimelineControls receives isPlaying, currentAge, and handlers from useAnimation
Globe fills the full viewport (100vw × 100vh); TimelineControls overlays on top
Clicking Play starts animation — plate polygons update as age advances
Dragging the scrubber seeks to the selected age and updates the globe immediately
Animation auto-stops at 750 Ma
Dev server shows the complete working experience with no console errors
`npx vite build` completes without errors
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Rewrite src/App.tsx. Import useAnimation from src/hooks/useAnimation.ts, usePlateData from src/hooks/usePlateData.ts, Globe from src/components/Globe.tsx, TimelineControls from src/components/TimelineControls.tsx. Layout: position: relative on a full-viewport div containing Globe; TimelineControls positioned absolute bottom. Pass globe data={plateData.data} and controls props from animation hook.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. App.tsx wires all hooks and components.

userStories.title: Polish — color-coded plates, AgeLabel, speed control
userStories.description: As a user, I want color-coded plate polygons and a cleaner UI with speed control so that the visualization is more legible and the experience feels polished.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/AgeLabel.tsx exists and renders the current age prominently (e.g., '250 Ma' large text, top-center of screen)
GeoJsonLayer in Globe.tsx uses filled: true with a semi-transparent fill color derived from PLATEID1 (hash-based color, consistent per plate)
TimelineControls includes a speed selector with at least 3 options: 10 Ma/s, 30 Ma/s, 100 Ma/s
Speed selection updates useAnimation speed — animation visibly changes pace
Globe background (ocean) uses a dark blue color (not white)
Plate polygon stroke remains visible over fill
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
`npx vite build` completes without errors
userStories.technicalNotes: Create src/components/AgeLabel.tsx. Modify src/components/Globe.tsx to add filled: true, getFillColor based on feature PLATEID1 (use (id * 2654435761) & 0xFFFFFF for hash coloring, with alpha ~80). Modify src/components/TimelineControls.tsx to add speed selector. Expose speed and setSpeed from useAnimation hook (add to return value). Dark ocean background: set DeckGL style={{ background: '#1a2744' }}. Files to modify: Globe.tsx, TimelineControls.tsx, useAnimation.ts. File to create: AgeLabel.tsx.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: AgeLabel.tsx (new), Globe.tsx, TimelineControls.tsx, useAnimation.ts, App.tsx (modified).
